rationale: 23 inbound TCP connections to RDP port 3389 on host-01.corp.local originate from a single external IP (23.93.242.200) over approximately 5.5 minutes, each using a new sequential source port — a pattern strongly consistent with an RDP brute-force or credential-stuffing attack (A0 through EV-022). The external IP is not a known corporate address and RDP should not be directly exposed to the internet in a typical enterprise environment. The volume, cadence, and sequential source-port increments rule out normal user activity and indicate automated tooling. No authentication success events, post-connection process activity, or lateral movement evidence is present in this package, so compromise cannot be confirmed — but the attack is actively ongoing and warrants immediate response.
